_note: Planet (Navagraha) mantra pages. Bija textDevanagari/transliteration MUST byte-match _canonical_bija.json (enforced by validate_mantra.mjs). Each planet carries up to 3 lineages: tantric bija, Vedic name, Navagraha Gayatri — labelled, never merged.

planets.whatIs: The Shani mantra is the seed (bija) invocation of Shani, the planet Saturn and son of Surya, the great teacher of discipline, justice and karmic consequence in Vedic astrology. Its principal tantric form, recorded in the Mantra Mahodadhi, is "Om Praam Preem Praum Sah Shanaischaraya Namah." Devotees chant it to steady the mind during Sade Sati and the dhaiya, to reduce the hardships Saturn brings when afflicted, and to earn the patience, endurance and honest labour Shani rewards. It is traditionally begun on a Saturday, faced toward the west, using an iron or blue-sapphire-substitute mala, and is associated with service to the needy, sesame oil, and the colour black.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ प्रां प्रीं प्रौं सः शनैश्चराय नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Praam Preem Praum Sah Shanaischaraya Namah
mantras.englishExplanation: The tantric seed mantra of Saturn; the bijas Praam-Preem-Praum with the bindu 'Sah' invoke Shani's disciplinary grace.
mantras.hindiExplanation: शनि का तांत्रिक बीज मंत्र; प्रां-प्रीं-प्रौं बीजाक्षर शनि की न्यायप्रिय कृपा का आवाहन करते हैं।

mantras.englishExplanation: The simple Vedic name mantra — a salutation to 'the slow-mover', suitable for daily practice without initiation.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ काकध्वजाय विद्महे खड्गहस्ताय धीमहि तन्नो मन्दः प्रचोदयात्
mantras.transliteration: Om Kakadhwajaya Vidmahe Khadgahastaya Dhimahi Tanno Mandah Prachodayat
mantras.englishExplanation: The 24-syllable Gayatri-metre invocation of Saturn. Epithets vary between compilations; the structure is classical.

benefits: Traditionally chanted to ease the trials of Sade Sati and Shani dhaiya
Cultivates patience, discipline, and steady effort
Associated with relief from delays, obstacles, and chronic difficulty when Saturn is afflicted
Supports justice, honest labour, and service to the underprivileged
vidhi.impact: Shani is the karaka of discipline and consequence. Regular japa is traditionally held to steady the mind and align the practitioner with patient, ethical effort rather than to magically remove karma.

mala.why: Iron and the colour black are traditionally associated with Saturn

vidhi.bestTime: Saturday at dusk, or during Shani hora; pradosh kaal is favoured

pujaModes.minimal: 108 japa of the name mantra facing west on Saturday
pujaModes.medium: 108 japa + sesame-oil lamp + offering to the needy
pujaModes.full: Sankalpa of 23,000 over 40 days with daily til-oil lamp, Saturday Shani puja, and anna-daan

guruDiksha.note: The simple name mantra needs no initiation; the tantric bija is best taken under a qualified guru.

vidhi.deityImageOrIdol: Image of Shani Dev (dark complexion, holding a staff, mounted on a crow or buffalo)

dos: Serve the elderly and the poor
donts: Do not chant with anger or to harm another
Avoid breaking the count midway without sankalpa
Do not skip the daily practice once a vow is taken

faqs.question: What is the Shani beej mantra?
faqs.answer: The Shani beej (seed) mantra is "Om Praam Preem Praum Sah Shanaischaraya Namah", recorded in the Mantra Mahodadhi. A simpler Vedic form is "Om Shanaischaraya Namah".

faqs.question: How many times should the Shani mantra be chanted?
faqs.answer: Traditionally 108 times daily, building to a sankalpa of 23,000 repetitions over 40 days for Saturn-related observances.

faqs.question: Which day is best for the Shani mantra?
faqs.answer: Saturday (Shanivar), the weekday ruled by Saturn, traditionally begun in the waning fortnight (Krishna Paksha) and chanted facing west.

planets.whatIs: The Surya mantra is the seed (bija) invocation of Surya, the Sun, the visible soul of the cosmos (pratyaksha devata) and source of vitality, health and authority in Vedic astrology. Its tantric form from the Mantra Mahodadhi is "Om Hraam Hreem Hraum Sah Suryaya Namah." Chanted at sunrise facing east, often with Arghya (a water offering to the rising sun), it is traditionally practised for radiant health, confidence, leadership, relief from eye and bone ailments, and the strengthening of a weak Sun in the birth chart. It belongs to the same Surya tradition as the Aditya Hridayam and the Gayatri, and is associated with copper, red flowers, wheat and the colour gold.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ ह्रां ह्रीं ह्रौं सः सूर्याय नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Hraam Hreem Hraum Sah Suryaya Namah
mantras.englishExplanation: The tantric seed mantra of the Sun; Hraam-Hreem-Hraum with 'Sah' invoke Surya's life-giving radiance.
mantras.hindiExplanation: सूर्य का तांत्रिक बीज मंत्र; ह्रां-ह्रीं-ह्रौं बीजाक्षर सूर्य की प्राणदायिनी ऊर्जा का आवाहन करते हैं।

mantras.englishExplanation: The simple Vedic name mantra of the Sun, recited at sunrise; needs no initiation.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ भास्कराय विद्महे महाद्युतिकराय धीमहि तन्नो आदित्यः प्रचोदयात्
mantras.transliteration: Om Bhaskaraya Vidmahe Mahadyutikaraya Dhimahi Tanno Adityah Prachodayat
mantras.englishExplanation: The 24-syllable Gayatri-metre invocation of the Sun.

benefits: Traditionally chanted for vitality, radiant health, and immunity
Associated with relief from eye, heart, and bone ailments when the Sun is weak
Supports a strong, well-placed Sun in the birth chart
vidhi.impact: Surya is the karaka of soul, vitality and authority. Sunrise japa with Arghya is traditionally held to invigorate body and will.

mala.why: Red and copper tones are associated with the Sun

vidhi.bestTime: Sunrise (Brahma Muhurta to early morning), during Surya hora
vidhi.bestDayToStart: Sunday (Ravivar), ideally Shukla Paksha or Ratha Saptami
vidhi.direction: Face East toward the rising sun

pujaModes.minimal: 108 japa of the name mantra at sunrise facing east, with water Arghya
pujaModes.full: Sankalpa of 7,000 over 40 days with daily sunrise Arghya and Aditya Hridayam recitation

guruDiksha.note: Name mantra and Arghya need no initiation; the bija is best taken from a guru.

vidhi.deityImageOrIdol: Image of Surya Dev on a seven-horse chariot driven by Aruna

dos: Offer Arghya with a copper vessel
Maintain cleanliness and a sattvic diet
donts: Do not chant after sunset for solar practice
Avoid offering Arghya with the water splashing on the feet
Do not skip the daily Arghya once vowed

faqs.question: What is the Surya beej mantra?
faqs.answer: The Surya beej (seed) mantra is "Om Hraam Hreem Hraum Sah Suryaya Namah" from the Mantra Mahodadhi. The simple Vedic form is "Om Suryaya Namah".

faqs.question: When should the Surya mantra be chanted?
faqs.answer: At sunrise, facing east, ideally on a Sunday, accompanied by Arghya (a water offering to the rising sun).

faqs.question: How many times should the Surya mantra be chanted?
faqs.answer: Traditionally 108 times daily, building toward a sankalpa of 7,000 repetitions over 40 days.

planets.whatIs: The Mangal mantra is the seed (bija) invocation of Mangal, the planet Mars, the commander of the celestial assembly and karaka of energy, courage, land and brothers in Vedic astrology. Its tantric form from the Mantra Mahodadhi is "Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaya Namah." It is chanted on Tuesdays facing south to channel Mars's drive constructively, to address Mangal dosha (the 'manglik' affliction) before marriage, to build courage and stamina, and to ease conflict, accidents and blood-related ailments when Mars is afflicted. It is closely linked with Hanuman worship and associated with red coral, red flowers, masoor dal and the colour red.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ क्रां क्रीं क्रौं सः भौमाय नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaya Namah
mantras.englishExplanation: The tantric seed mantra of Mars; Kraam-Kreem-Kraum with 'Sah' invoke Mangal's courageous energy.
mantras.hindiExplanation: मंगल का तांत्रिक बीज मंत्र; क्रां-क्रीं-क्रौं बीजाक्षर मंगल की साहसिक ऊर्जा का आवाहन करते हैं।

mantras.englishExplanation: The simple Vedic name mantra of Mars (Bhauma, the son of the Earth).

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ अङ्गारकाय विद्महे शक्तिहस्ताय धीमहि तन्नो भौमः प्रचोदयात्
mantras.transliteration: Om Angarakaya Vidmahe Shaktihastaya Dhimahi Tanno Bhaumah Prachodayat
mantras.englishExplanation: The 24-syllable Gayatri-metre invocation of Mars (Angaraka, the ember-hued one).

benefits: Traditionally chanted to address Mangal dosha (manglik) before marriage
Builds courage, stamina, drive, and decisiveness
Associated with relief from conflict, accidents, and blood-related issues when Mars is afflicted
Channels Mars's aggression into disciplined action
vidhi.impact: Mangal is the karaka of energy and courage. Tuesday japa, often alongside Hanuman worship, is traditionally held to temper and direct Mars's force.

mala.why: Red coral is the gemstone of Mars

pujaModes.minimal: 108 japa of the name mantra facing south on Tuesday
pujaModes.full: Sankalpa of 10,000 over 40 days with Tuesday Mangal/Hanuman puja and red-item daan

guruDiksha.note: Name mantra is open; the bija is best taken under a guru, especially for dosha remediation.

vidhi.deityImageOrIdol: Image of Mangal Dev (red complexion, four arms) or Hanuman for Mars remedies

dos: Direct the energy into constructive effort
donts: Do not chant in anger or to dominate others
Avoid non-vegetarian food and alcohol during the vow
Do not abandon the count midway

faqs.question: What is the Mangal beej mantra?
faqs.answer: The Mangal beej (seed) mantra is "Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaya Namah" from the Mantra Mahodadhi. The simple Vedic form is "Om Bhaumaya Namah".

faqs.question: Which mantra is best for Manglik dosha?
faqs.answer: The Mangal mantra chanted on Tuesdays, traditionally combined with Hanuman worship (Hanuman Chalisa), is the classical observance for Mangal (manglik) dosha. Consult an astrologer for chart-specific guidance.

faqs.question: Which day and direction for the Mangal mantra?
faqs.answer: Tuesday (Mangalvar), facing south, the day and direction associated with Mars.
